question.text: A fund manager hedges a Rs. 90 lakh equity portfolio (beta 1.3) using Nifty futures at 17,700 with lot size 50. How many contracts should be shorted?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Number of contracts = (Beta × Portfolio Value) / (Futures Price × Lot Size) = (1.3 × 90,00,000) / (17,700 × 50) = 1,17,00,000 / 8,85,000 = 13.22 ≈ 13 contracts. **The Trap:** 10 contracts ignores beta (90,00,000 / 8,85,000 = 10.17). Since beta > 1, the portfolio is more volatile than the index and needs more contracts. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch5 covers this exact calculation. Series XIII compares it with duration-based hedge ratio in bond markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always round to the nearest whole number of contracts — fractional contracts cannot be traded. Slight over/under-hedging is unavoidable.
